evidenceChecked: content/010-orientation.md#The endpoint is not the change
content/020-concept.md#A graph is a set of input-output claims
content/030-worked-example.md#Transform a parent rule and verify key points
content/040-misconception.md#Inside changes run opposite to the sign
content/050-practice.md#Move among rule, table, and graph language

findings.finding: The central definition and repeated use of average rate of change are not represented by any claim. The formula and its interpretation appear in scenes orientation, concept, worked-example, practice, transfer, and synthesis, but the three declared claims cover only function notation/composition, axis labeling, and transformation inference. Consequently, the same-scene source notes do not expose evidence for a material quantitative claim that is also a declared objective and assessed mastery requirement.
findings.resolution: Add a stable claim for the definition, conditions, units, and interpretation of average rate of change; attach an authoritative source with an exact supporting locator; map the claim to every scene that teaches or reapplies it; and update each affected source note with the complete claim and source IDs.

findings.finding: The recorded locator does not match the cited source. DLMF section 1.4 is a calculus-of-one-variable reference organized around monotonicity, continuity, derivatives, integrals, maxima, and related topics; direct inspection found no definition of a function, domain treatment, composite-function definition, or graph-transformation rules matching the locator. It therefore does not substantiate claim-function-notation or the premises used for claim-transform-inference, despite those claims supporting nearly every scene.
findings.resolution: Replace or supplement this record with authoritative sources that explicitly support the introductory function/domain/composition definitions and algebraic graph transformations. Give exact section locators, then update claims.json and all affected source notes so each source is attached only to claims it actually supports.

findings.finding: The mastery gate does not fully test its declared objectives. objective-interpret-function promises determination of range, but no range task is mapped to that objective; item-transfer-quadratic asks for a range but is mapped to other objectives. objective-read-graph promises interpretation of intervals and axis scales, yet no mapped item presents a non-unit, offset, or otherwise consequential axis scale, and no item requires an interval interpretation. A learner can therefore pass without demonstrating all performances asserted by the objective mappings.
findings.resolution: Add or remap assessment evidence so range is explicitly mapped to objective-interpret-function, and add tasks that require interpreting interval behavior and a consequential labeled axis scale. Keep the answer logic and rubrics sensitive to scale and interval errors.

findings.finding: The statement that f(x-h)+k shifts right by h and up by k omits sign conditions. As written for unrestricted h and k, it is false when either parameter is negative; negative h produces a left shift and negative k produces a downward shift.
findings.resolution: State the rule conditionally, for example right by h when h is positive and left by |h| when h is negative, with the analogous qualification for k.

findings.finding: Independent recalculation confirms the keyed transformations, maximum (-2,8), range p(x) ≤ 8, intercepts x = -6 and x = 2, average rate -1 from -2 to 0, and the proposed verification table. The other numerical and algebraic answer keys were also recalculated without finding a mathematical error.
findings.resolution: No change required to these calculations or keyed results.

limitations: The requested scope prohibited access to site/, so the graph-node definition, cross-lesson terminology ledger, quorum policy, and review schema were not independently inspected. Outcome scope was judged from lesson.json and the complete candidate pack.

No repository validation command was run because it would execute prohibited scripts and site paths, and this read-only review did not save an artifact. Structural validation is not evidence for this verdict.

Both declared sources were directly accessible and neither was marked human-only. The NIST SI Guide section 7.1 supported claim-axis-quantity; the cited DLMF section did not support its recorded locator.
No conflict of interest was identified. The review competence applied here is introductory real-variable functions, graph interpretation, rates of change, and algebraic transformations.
